headlines.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

headlines.title: Acting Attorney General Blanche testifies at House hearing
headlines.description: Watch live coverage as Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche testifies before a House Appropriations Subcommittee hearing.

headlines.title: Voters Head to Polls in Key States, Setting the Stage for Midterms
headlines.description: Voters are gearing up for a key primaries with razor-tight races in six states, including Iowa where Democrats are hoping to win a Senate seat in the traditionally Republican state. Meanwhile in California, former reality star Spencer Pratt is gaining late momentum in his push to lead Los Angeles, the nation’s second-largest city. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Russia targets Kyiv with massive overnight attack
headlines.description: More than a dozen people were killed and hundreds more inured after an overnight barrage of Russian missiles and drones targeted the Ukrainian capital. Russia had previously warned that it intended “systematic strikes” on targets in Kyiv linked to Ukraine’s military, urging foreigners to leave.

headlines.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade new attacks despite Trump promise of de-escalation
headlines.description: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks

headlines.title: Protests erupt over U.S. Ebola quarantine center in Kenya
headlines.description: Hundreds of people gathered in Nanyuki, Kenya, to protest a U.S.-proposed Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus. Kenya does not have any suspected Ebola cases, but neighboring Uganda has reported nine and closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

headlines.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory
headlines.description: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

headlines.title: N.J. targets prison company running immigration detention center in lawsuits over conditions
headlines.description: The mayor of Newark is doubling down on his efforts to shut down Delaney Hall, the New Jersey immigration detention facility that has been at the center of protests for nearly two weeks, drawing demonstrators over allegations of poor conditions at the facility and counterprotesters voicing support for ICE

headlines.title: From The Sports Desk: Heavyweights Carolina, Vegas meet in Stanley Cup Final
headlines.description: Caught up in the NBA playoffs?

headlines.title: Trump to speak at the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Dinner
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday that he would attend the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in July after the spring event was abruptly cut short when a gunman ran through a security checkpoint.

headlines.title: NYC officials warn of ‘extremely dangerous’ sewers after mystery men emerge from manholes
headlines.description: Mystery men who are “up to no good” have been roaming through New York City’s vast sewer system, and city officials on Tuesday warned potential copycats of dangers they’d face exploring Gotham’s treacherous underground

headlines.title: Trump signs order seeking early access to powerful AI models before release
headlines.description: President Trump signed an order seeking the establishment of a voluntary framework for AI companies to provide the government early access to new models.
